obligations.reason: Visible scope contains external JTAG pins, TAP DMI access signals, DMI request/response CDC, password/hash-related unlock signals, and debug-module outputs; the RTL should be checked to ensure DMI access is actually gated by authorization before reaching protected debug capabilities.
obligations.subject: external JTAG requester through tck_i/tms_i/td_i
obligations.operation: issue read/write DMI transactions and trigger debug actions
obligations.object: debug module DMI interface and debug capabilities (dmi_req_valid_o/dmi_req, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o)
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG/DMI requests that can access debug module functionality must be mediated by successful authentication/unlock state (e.g., jtag_unlock/pass_check/hashValid) and any required debug authorization, not only by TAP selection/update signals.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG pins/tap -> dmi_jtag -> dmi_cdc -> debug module DMI request outputs in riscv_peripherals
state_conditions_to_check: reset state of unlock/pass mode
transition that sets jtag_unlock or pass_check
whether dmi_access/update_dr can assert request valid while locked
whether authorization persists or clears across dmi_reset/trst/rst
required_evidence_slots: subject: external JTAG/TAP requester
operation: DMI read/write or debug trigger
object: debug module request/control signal
expected guard: jtag_unlock/pass_check/hashValid or equivalent authorization
observed RTL path and gating conditions
source locations for TAP update, request-valid generation, and top-level wiring
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists relevant signals but not full procedural assignments, so inspect request-valid generation and unlock gating in the RTL.

obligations.reason: The scope includes fuse_mem with both jtag_hash_o and rdata_o plus top-level fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata wiring; check whether the same authentication material can be read by software or network requesters without privilege or lock checks.
obligations.subject: software/MMIO/NoC requester reaching fuse_mem req_i
obligations.operation: read/expose authentication hash or fuse words
obligations.expected_guard: Fuse/hash contents used for debug authentication should not be readable or exposed through an unprivileged MMIO/fuse read path; reads should be limited by privilege/access-control/lock mediation if the value is security-sensitive.
state_conditions_to_check: reset/initialization contents of fuse memory
address ranges that select hash/fuse words
whether req_i read is conditioned on priv_lvl_i, acc_ctrl, reglk_ctrl, or debug unlock
whether jtag_hash_o is continuously exposed internally to any external read mux
required_evidence_slots: subject: requester driving fuse_req/addr_i
operation: read fuse memory/hash words
object: jtag_hash or fuse rdata entries
expected guard: privilege/access-control/lock or non-readable policy
observed read mux/address decode behavior
source locations for fuse instantiation and rdata assignment
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect truncated top-level decode around fuse_req and fuse_mem internals to determine exact exposure path.

obligations.reason: Top-level declares reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl and derives acc_ctrl_c; verify both the policy registers and every use site are mediated, and that lock state is not just present but actually enforced on all write paths.
obligations.subject: software-visible register interface / NoC requester
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl policy registers and protected peripheral control paths
obligations.expected_guard: Writes to access-control and register-lock configuration must require an authorized privilege level and, once locked, lock bits should prevent subsequent writes to protected configuration and protected peripherals.
obligations.candidate_path: NoC/MMIO requester -> riscv_peripherals address decode -> reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl configuration/use sites
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults for acc_ctrl and reglk_ctrl
set/clear behavior of lock bits
whether any write can clear lock bits after set
whether acc_ctrl_c aliasing for peripheral index j==5 preserves intended policy
debug/test/DMA bypasses around lock enforcement
required_evidence_slots: subject: MMIO/NoC/configuration requester
operation: write policy register or protected register
object: reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl and protected peripheral enables
expected guard: machine privilege/authorized access plus sticky lock
observed write enables and use-site gating
source locations for assignments/procedural updates and peripheral decodes
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows signals and one derived assignment but not full register update logic.

obligations.reason: The top-level exposes many NoC request/response ports for crypto, debug, reset, DMA, accounting and lock peripherals; check address decode and routing so unauthorized requesters cannot reach protected regions through missing/overlapping range checks.
obligations.subject: network/MMIO requester on buf_ariane_*_noc2 inputs
obligations.operation: read/write peripheral registers or route transactions
obligations.object: security-sensitive peripheral MMIO regions and NoC response paths (AES, RSA, RNG, SHA256, HMAC, debug, reset, DMA, fuse/reglk/acct)
obligations.expected_guard: Access to security-sensitive peripheral address regions should be gated by the configured access-control matrix and requester privilege/attributes; decode overlaps or default routes must not bypass acc_ctrl/reglk policy.
obligations.candidate_path: NoC ports for AES/RSA/RNG/SHA/HMAC/DMA/debug/reset/etc. -> riscv_peripherals decoders and acc_ctrl_c checks
state_conditions_to_check: range boundaries and overlaps
default case behavior for unmapped addresses
read-vs-write guard consistency
whether response data is returned when access is denied
effect of reset defaults of access-control bits
required_evidence_slots: subject: NoC/MMIO requester
operation: read/write transaction
object: peripheral region/register
expected guard: acc_ctrl_c/privilege/address-range mediation
observed decode conditions and response mux
path/reachability from port to object
source locations for decoders and valid/ready generation
obligations.uncertainty: Detailed decoders are in the larger top file beyond visible fact excerpt.

obligations.reason: TAP has testmode_i and a DFT clock mux; the analysis should verify test mode only affects clocking/test behavior and does not bypass authentication or force DMI access/output enable.
obligations.subject: test/JTAG requester using testmode_i and TAP pins
obligations.operation: shift/capture/update DMI data or select DMI chain in test mode
obligations.object: DMI scan chain / TDO output / debug request path
obligations.expected_guard: Test mode or DFT clock selection must not enable unauthorized DMI access or expose DMI response data on TDO unless the normal debug authorization and TAP state checks are satisfied.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG TAP testmode_i/DFT clock mux -> dmi_access/dmi_tdi/dmi_tdo and td_o/tdo_oe_o
state_conditions_to_check: testmode_i asserted vs deasserted behavior
trst_ni/test_logic_reset effects
whether tdo_oe_o is enabled for protected chains while locked
whether dmi_access_o ignores authorization in test mode
required_evidence_slots: subject: test/JTAG interface
operation: select/shift/capture DMI chain
object: dmi_access_o, dmi_tdo_i->td_o, request-valid path
expected guard: TAP state plus debug authorization/unlock
observed testmode mux behavior and output enable gating
source locations for clock mux, tdo mux, and DMI select logic
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect TAP state machine and top-level conditional compilation/wiring around TDO assignments.

obligations.reason: Signals indicate a password/HMAC-based JTAG unlock flow. Check that all transitions to unlocked state depend on a valid digest comparison and cannot be reached by reset defaults, stale hashValid, pass_mode, we_flag, or bypass controls.
obligations.subject: external JTAG authentication requester
obligations.expected_guard: Unlock should be granted only after HMAC computation is complete and compared against the provisioned jtag_hash; bypass modes such as key_hash_bypass_i or pass_mode must be authorized and not allow unconditional unlock.
obligations.candidate_path: dmi_jtag password/HMAC logic (pass_data, exp_hash, hashValid, pass_check, pass_mode, startHash) -> jtag_unlock_o/request gating
state_conditions_to_check: reset values of pass_check/pass_mode/hashValid/jtag_unlock
conditions setting and clearing unlock
whether hashValid can remain stale across new pass_data
effect of key_hash_bypass_i in hmac instance
influence of we_flag and priv_lvl_i on authentication flow
required_evidence_slots: subject: JTAG requester providing password/data
operation: set unlock/authenticated state
object: jtag_unlock_o/pass_check/pass_mode
expected guard: HMAC ready + valid digest equals jtag_hash_i under authorized mode
observed FSM/assignment conditions
source locations for HMAC instance and unlock assignment
obligations.uncertainty: Facts expose signal names and instance, but exact FSM conditions require RTL inspection.

obligations.reason: Signals for a debug master port are present and some grant/response assignments appear tied to zero. Verify whether the debug module can nevertheless issue requests to any fabric and, if so, that address-range and authorization checks apply.
obligations.subject: debug module acting as bus master
obligations.operation: debug-initiated read/write of system address space
obligations.object: system memory/peripheral fabric reachable from debug module master
obligations.expected_guard: If debug master access is enabled, memory/peripheral accesses should be restricted by debug authorization and address/range policy; if tied off, confirm no alternate path bypasses the tie-off.
obligations.candidate_path: debug module master signals dm_master_req/add/we/wdata -> SoC/peripheral/memory fabric or tied-off grants
state_conditions_to_check: whether dm_master_req/we are consumed anywhere
whether grants are permanently zero in all build configurations
whether alternative debug slave paths can trigger master-like access
reset/active state of dmactive_o
required_evidence_slots: subject: debug module master
operation: memory/peripheral read/write
object: system address space or protected peripherals
expected guard: debug authorization plus address/range permission
observed connectivity or tie-off
source locations for dm_master assignments and any bridge instantiation
obligations.uncertainty: Fact excerpt shows tie-offs for response/grant but not all connectivity/conditional compilation.

obligations.reason: Visible reset-control signals and debug reset outputs suggest software/JTAG may affect reset/lifecycle state; verify reset controls cannot be invoked by unauthorized requesters and do not leave authentication or locks in unsafe states.
obligations.subject: software-visible reset interface and/or JTAG debug interface
obligations.operation: assert/reset peripheral, core, or debug state
obligations.object: reset control signals rst_1..rst_13, ndmreset_o, dmi_rst_no and security state affected by reset
obligations.expected_guard: Reset or lifecycle-control operations affecting harts/peripherals/debug state should require privilege/access-control or debug authorization, and reset should clear sensitive auth/lock/session state safely without clearing sticky protections unexpectedly.
obligations.candidate_path: reset-control NoC/MMIO path and debug DMI reset signals -> rst_1..rst_13, ndmreset_o, dmi_rst_no
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults for lock/access-control/authentication state
which resets clear jtag_unlock/pass_check/hashValid
whether reset path can clear reglk_ctrl after lock
whether ndmreset_o/dmi_rst_no can be asserted while locked
required_evidence_slots: subject: MMIO/NoC or JTAG requester
operation: assert reset or clear state
object: hart/peripheral/debug reset controls and security registers
expected guard: privilege/access-control/debug authorization
observed reset generation and affected state
source locations for reset register updates and output assignments
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect top-level reset decode around rst_* signals and DMI reset handling.

obligations.reason: The scope has ariane_dma NoC ports and policy signals; check DMA request/config paths for access-control mediation and for consistency between configuration-time and transfer-time checks.
obligations.subject: DMA engine or software configuring DMA via NoC/MMIO
obligations.operation: configure DMA or perform DMA read/write transfer
obligations.object: DMA registers/transactions and protected memory/peripheral regions
obligations.expected_guard: DMA configuration and active DMA transfers should be restricted by privilege/access-control and should not bypass protected address regions or permit races where policy changes during active transfer evade checks.
obligations.candidate_path: DMA NoC port/configuration -> memory/peripheral accesses and acc_ctrl/reglk policy
state_conditions_to_check: whether DMA can target debug/fuse/crypto/register-lock regions
policy updates during active transfer
reset defaults of DMA enable/configuration
whether denied accesses return data or complete writes
required_evidence_slots: subject: requester programming DMA
operation: DMA read/write transfer
object: protected address ranges/peripherals
expected guard: privilege/access-control/range check at config and use site
observed DMA port decode and policy use
source locations for DMA NoC routing/config registers
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists DMA ports but not DMA internals; obligation is to inspect visible top-level mediation.

obligations.reason: The HMAC module has key input, bypass input, internal pad/digest state, and output hash_o; inspect whether outputs are only used for comparison/authentication and not routed to TDO/MMIO in a way that exposes secret-derived state beyond intended digest behavior.
obligations.subject: external observer through JTAG/TDO or software-visible interface
obligations.object: HMAC key/input-derived internal state and hash output used for authentication
obligations.expected_guard: Secret key material used by HMAC should not be directly exposed on outputs or read muxes; bypass controls should not transform secret-dependent state into externally readable data without authorization.
obligations.candidate_path: HMAC module key_i/message_i/key_hash_bypass_i -> hash_o/ready_o and dmi_jtag unlock use
state_conditions_to_check: key_hash_bypass_i asserted behavior
ready/hash valid timing and stale digest retention
reset clearing of digest/intermediate registers
whether pass_data/message_i is attacker-controlled while key is secret
required_evidence_slots: subject: JTAG or software observer
operation: read/expose hash or internal state
object: key_i, ipad/opad, sha_idigest, hash_o
expected guard: no external read path except intended authorized comparison/output
observed dataflow to dmi_jtag/TDO/read mux
source locations for hash_o assignments and consumers
obligations.uncertainty: May be benign if hash_o only feeds comparison; verify dataflow rather than assuming exposure.
